Issue in the name and type of Alexander the Great, struck in Pella (Amphipolis) c. 276/5-273/2 BC Head of young Herakles in lion skin headdress. Reverse:  Zeus enthroned left holding eagle and scepter. Monogram to l. and under throne. HGC Vol. 3, 1040; Price 574-603. NGC graded Choice XF, Strike: 5/5, Surface: 3/5. sales.description:
Thrace, Mesembria. Ca, 125-65 BC. Silver Tetradrachm (16.44g) 29 mm. Struck in the types of Alexander the Great. Heracles wearing lion-skin headdress to r. Reverse: Zeus seated l. holding eagle, Corinthian helmet and monogram below. NGC AU; Strike: 4/5, Surface: 4/5. Excellent metal and lustrous, lightly toned. An unusually choice example for this late style tetradrachm.  Estimated Value $650 - UP
Ex The Peter Preovolos Collection....
